title: Young Adult Resting State and Auditory Oddball Task EEG Data
description: This dataset comprises electroencephalography (EEG) recordings from young adult participants during resting state and auditory oddball task conditions. Data were collected under two experimental conditions: a control condition and a shock threat condition involving threat of unpredictable shock. The dataset includes both passive and active variants of the auditory oddball task, as well as resting state recordings with eyes open and closed, designed to investigate locus coeruleus-noradrenaline system function and its relationship to attention and arousal.
methods_description: EEG data were collected during resting state (5 minutes eyes open and 5 minutes eyes closed) and auditory oddball tasks (passive and active variants) under control and shock threat conditions. The auditory oddball task included standard tones, target tones, and distractor tones, with the active variant including a manual response component.
